title: Suspect Donations and Unidentified Sources of the Ruling Civil Contract's Election Campaign Finances
content: Political parties are required to submit their annual reports to the Corruption Prevention Commission (CPC) by May 31 of the next reporting year. The Civil Contract party did not disclose the identities of 987 donors throughout the pre-election period. Despite requests from Infocom and several other media outlets, the party consistently declined to provide such information. Approximately five months after the elections, on December 22, the party finally shared the names of the donors, after the Freedom of Information Center of Armenia (FOICA) initiated legal action. Among the 140 donors who contributed 2.5 million AMD, at least 8 are employed by Yeremyan Projects. Infocom reported having internal information that the donations were not made from the personal funds of the employees. Syuzanna Sayadyan, a member of the Council of Elders of the Artashat community, contributed 2.5 million AMD, yet her annual income for 2022 amounted to only 702,975 AMD. 87% of the total money transferred to Civil Contract came from fewer than 200 people, the majority of whom donated about $6,200, the maximum campaign contribution under Armenian law.

title: ՔՊ նախdelays delays delays delays delays delays delays delays delays delays
content: Armenian-language version of the investigation into the Civil Contract party's suspicious pre-election donation transactions and unidentified sources of finances. The investigation found unusual patterns in the ruling party's list of donors, with several individuals from donor lists claiming they had never donated money to the party.

title: Armenian universities remain under political control (Infocom investigation)
content: Despite promises to eliminate political influence from universities, Armenia's government has maintained significant control over the country's higher education institutions. The investigation found that 8 out of 12 chairs of state university boards of trustees in Yerevan are political appointees, with three being ruling party members. This starkly contrasts with Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan's 2018 pledge that no party member should have the right to be a university rector. 47% of the Prime Minister's appointees have political exposure, while an additional 18.3% of ministerial appointees are either state officials or connected to the ruling party.

title: Kajaran community made purchases of more than 700 million drams from a company with the participation of the mayor's wife
content: Investigative piece about procurement practices in the Kajaran community involving a company connected to the mayor's wife and a kindergarten construction contract worth over 700 million AMD.

title: Canada, Great Britain and France have called on Israel to allow humanitarian aid to reach Gaza
content: Canada, Great Britain, and France have called on Israel to cease military operations in Gaza and immediately allow humanitarian aid to reach the region. Source: 1lurer.am.

title: Anti-Corruption Court president's wife did not submit declarations; she says the reason is technical
content: The wife of the Anti-Corruption Court president has not submitted asset declarations. According to her, the reason is technical.

title: Missing data and errors in the declarations of the Head of the Prosecutor's Office Illegal Property Confiscation Department
content: Investigative article about missing data and discrepancies found in the asset declarations submitted by the head of the Prosecutor's Office department responsible for illegal property confiscation.

title: Karen Vanetsyan has been detained for two months
content: Karen Vanetsyan has been detained for a period of two months. Source: factor.am.

title: USAID plans to allocate about $8.5 million for forcibly displaced persons from Nagorno-Karabakh
content: The U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) plans to allocate approximately $8.5 million for forcibly displaced persons from Nagorno-Karabakh. Source: armenpress.am.

title: The Black Sea has turned black: ecological disaster in the Black Sea
content: Report on an ecological disaster in the Black Sea region. Source: hetq.am.

title: Opposition has no final decision yet over nominating candidate for President of Armenia
content: The opposition Armenia and I Have the Honor factions of the Parliament continue discussions over the issue of whether to nominate a candidate for the President of the Republic. MP from the Armenia faction Gegham Manukyan told Armenpress that discussions continue. Secretary of the I Have the Honor faction Hayk Mamijanyan said the faction does not have enough lawmakers for nominating a candidate but is holding consultations with the Armenia faction. Source: armenpress.am.

title: Our goal is to prevent any new concessions at any cost - Ishkhan Saghatelyan
content: Ishkhan Saghatelyan, Vice President of the RA National Assembly, discusses rally plans and political developments, stating the goal of preventing new concessions.

title: Nikol Pashinyan spoke at the UN about the return of Armenian POWs, unblocking roads and establishing peace in the region
content: Coverage of the speech of the Prime Minister of Armenia at the General Debate of the UN General Assembly. Azerbaijan still holds several dozen citizens of Armenia in captivity and has sentenced many to 6-20 years imprisonment. Armenia stated it would consistently advance the peace agenda. Pashinyan considered the opening of regional communications extremely important as stated in the trilateral declaration.

title: Nikol Pashinyan meets with Maia Sandu - the sides highlight development of economic relations between Armenia and Moldova
content: Pashinyan highlighted the development of relations between Armenia and Moldova, stressing the need to develop economic cooperation with good preconditions and necessary potential.

title: The Prime Minister of Armenia met with the President of Slovenia
content: Within the framework of the 77th session of the UN General Assembly, PM Pashinyan held bilateral meetings including with the President of Slovenia, Borut Pahor.

title: RA SRC chief tax inspector arrested per the Anti-Corruption Committee
content: The Anti-Corruption Committee reported the arrest of a chief tax inspector from the State Revenue Committee.

title: Foreign Minister of Armenia Ararat Mirzoyan received the Secretary General of BSEC Lazar Comanescu
content: On February 23, Foreign Minister of Armenia Ararat Mirzoyan received the Secretary General of the Organization of the Black Sea Economic Cooperation (BSEC) Lazar Comanescu. Minister Mirzoyan expressed conviction that BSEC objectives based on depoliticized, inclusive regional economic cooperation and the inadmissibility of blockade policies are important for regional stability and peace.

title: SpaceX sent tourists to space for the first time; they returned safely
content: SpaceX sent tourists to space for the first time. They returned safely. Source: hetq.am.

title: 30 employees needed for total control of procurement processes; the state cannot afford such a resource - Karen Mnatskanyan
content: Karen Mnatskanyan discusses that 30 employees would be needed for total oversight of procurement processes, stating the state cannot afford such resources.

title: Mikayel Minasyan was the de facto shareholder of 49% of the Spayka company - SRC
content: The State Revenue Committee (SRC) reported that Mikayel Minasyan was the de facto shareholder of 49 percent of the Spayka company.

title: The government addressed the Catholicos statement on changing Kocharyan's preventive measure
content: The government has responded to the Catholicos (Supreme Patriarch) statement regarding changing the preventive measure for Robert Kocharyan. Source: azatutyun.am.

title: Infocom analysis: Armenian political elite's perception of Russia
content: An analysis of the Armenian political elite's perception of Russia. Variations in discourse are caused by times of crisis, including references to PM Pashinyan's speeches about Russia's role in mediation.

title: Daily News Summary collection
content: Daily news summaries covering the Armenia-Azerbaijan peace process, regional geopolitics, and EU relations. Iran's Foreign Minister believes the 3+3 format can settle tensions in the region. The Cabinet approved ratification of Armenia-Georgia simplified travel requirements. The EU-Armenia Parliamentary Partnership Committee called on Azerbaijan to immediately withdraw from the territories of Armenia.

title: Fact-checking section
content: Infocom sends written requests to government ministries to verify problematic claims in articles published by other Armenian media. This is the fact-checking section of the site.
